question.text: Sunita's monthly expenses are Rs.50,000. Of this, Rs.30,000 is household expenses and she expects Rs.10,000 in discretionary spending post-retirement. If inflation is 6% and she retires in 25 years, what will be her estimated monthly expense at retirement?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Post-retirement monthly expense = Rs.30,000 (household) + Rs.10,000 (discretionary) = Rs.40,000 in today's terms. Inflation-adjusted at retirement = Rs.40,000 x (1.06)^25 = Rs.40,000 x 4.29187 = Rs.1,71,675. **The Trap:** Some candidates may inflate the full Rs.50,000 instead of the post-retirement Rs.40,000. The question specifically asks for post-retirement expenses. **Cross-Exam Context:** This directly maps to the textbook example. Understanding expense categorization (mandatory, essential, discretionary) is key to accurate retirement planning. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Help clients distinguish between expenses that will reduce after retirement (commute, work clothes) and those that will increase (healthcare, leisure).
